title: Coelorachis impressa
taxonomy: Coelorachis impressa is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Andropogoneae and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Coelorachis impressa includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Liliopsida, and class as Liliopsida. This grass is also known as a type of perennial, evergreen, or annual plant depending on its growth conditions. Its classification is essential in understanding the plant's characteristics and its relationship with other species in the Poaceae family.

intro: Coelorachis impressa, also known as the impressed spike-grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Coelorachis impressa is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate various health conditions.
